summary: The dark-mode design conforms to both guides. Backend: it adds zero business surface (cookie-based, no model/controller/service/migration) because there is no business action — presentation state only — which aligns with backend-guide 'business logic lives in POROs' rather than violating it; ThemeHelper#dark_theme? is correctly a view helper, and testing is Minitest (helper unit test) plus a system test for the critical flow. UI: it reserves the brand accent (indigo-600) and semantic status colors, tokenizing only neutrals; the StatusHelper change preserves hue->meaning mapping in both themes and keeps the status word so color never carries meaning alone; it uses a semantic-token layer instead of hand-rolled per-view dark: utilities, which is exactly what ui-style-guide directs ('Dark mode later — don't hand-roll it per view'); the toggle is a trivial local toggle (permitted optimistic UI); the icon button carries aria-label + tooltip; WCAG AA (D7) matches the a11y baseline; and it includes the CLAUDE.md-required guide update in the same PR. All factual claims about existing files (StatusHelper as the single source of truth for status color, importmap pin_all_from controllers, raw gray/white/indigo utilities in the layouts and sidebar) were verified against the codebase. Minor advisory only (not blocking): §4.4's a11y attribute list for the toggle omits the mandatory visible focus ring (focus-visible:ring-2 ring-indigo-600, ui-style-guide §Accessibility), but this is already required by the guide the Implementer must follow.
